title: Use finalized candidate identity in supply-chain evidence
outOfScope: Publishing or tagging v0.5.39, creating a provider release, or dispatching release workflows.
Changing public distribution semantics or weakening fail-closed checks.
Quick Install and unrelated planned Work Items.
A separate budget-only Work Item or rewriting historical archive evidence.

sources.reason: Candidate source identity fallback currently disagrees with finalized evidence.
Concrete finalized candidate identity showing the mismatch.
Source resolution and release-evidence regression coverage.

riskAssessment.reason: Candidate-mode evidence must honor a finalized concrete identity without allowing stale or forged identity fields.

executionDecision.reason: User authorized stopping publication to correct the root release-identity process before continuing.

preReviewWarnings: Review precedence between finalized release-digests identity and historical provenance fallback.
Review stale identity rejection and unpublished distribution barrier.
acceptance: When finalizer has written a concrete candidate release-digests identity for the current release tag, supply-chain generation uses that identity tuple.
Candidate-mode fallback still reuses historical provenance only when no concrete finalized identity exists.
Regression tests prove post-finalizer release evidence passes and stale/mismatched identity remains fail-closed.
Formatting, budget, AI Finish, archive, PR, merge, and ai-close-work-item complete; no tag or release is created.
guidelines: Preserve candidate versus published evidence distinction.
Do not weaken distribution or rewrite immutable evidence.

intent.problem: After PR #308, finalizer binds release-digests to merge commit 52e50cf..., but candidate supply-chain fallback still resolves provenance commit 21c193..., so release evidence fails after a successful finalization.
intent.rationale: The finalized candidate identity must be the authoritative input for all downstream evidence generators; historical provenance is only a fallback before finalization.

rawUserRequest: 发现发布流程问题后停止发布，继续做根本流程修复，扫清发布版本之前的障碍；不要重复 CI/PR 循环，不发布 tag。

declaredIntent.summary: Make candidate supply-chain evidence consume the concrete identity emitted by finalizer while preserving fail-closed and unpublished-release gates.

restrictedWriteApproval.reason: User explicitly authorized root-cause process correction before continuing publication.

checkpointPolicy.reason: Record pre-edit and pre-finish evidence for a medium-risk release identity change.

scenarioCoverage.scenario: After finalizer writes a concrete candidate identity, supply-chain generation uses that same tuple.
Before a concrete finalized identity exists, candidate mode retains the historical provenance fallback.
Distribution remains blocked while v0.5.39 is not publicly published.

rollbackNote: Revert this Work Item diff and restore the previous candidate identity resolver and tests.

repaymentRecords: release_finalizer_identity_sync|archiveGrowth|519|520|user|2026-07-24|Reserve this functional root-process correction archive in the same Work Item; repay during final plan cleanup, not through a budget-only Work Item.
